key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ qù guò nǎ xiē dìfāng?

key_sentences.chinese: 我去过北京，那里的历史文化让我印象很深。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ qù guò Běijīng, nàlǐ de lìshǐ wénhuà ràng wǒ yìnxiàng hěn shēn.
key_sentences.english: I've been to Beijing; the history and culture there impressed me deeply.

key_sentences.chinese: 这次旅游真的很难忘，值得再去一次。
key_sentences.pinyin: Zhè cì lǚyóu zhēn de hěn nánwàng, zhíde zài qù yī cì.
key_sentences.english: This trip was truly unforgettable, worth going again.

key_sentences.chinese: 我打算明年去上海，你有什么建议吗？
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ dǎsuàn míngnián qù Shànghǎi, nǐ yǒu shénme jiànyì ma?
key_sentences.english: I plan to go to Shanghai next year; do you have any suggestions?

grammar_point.title: 过 (guò) — experiential aspect
grammar_point.explanation: Adding 过 after a verb indicates past experience — something you have done at least once. 我去过 = I have been there (before). The negative is 没去过 = have never been. This is different from 了 which just marks completion. 让 (ràng) means 'make/let': 让我高兴 = make me happy.

examples.english: I have eaten Peking duck before

writing_practice.stroke_order_note: Practice writing these characters focusing on stroke order. 过 = 3 parts: 寸 radical on right + walk radical on left. Start from top-left.

quiz.question: What does 过 (guò) indicate after a verb?
How do you say 'I plan to'?
